message:
perf(ft-kernel-cpu): pairwise summation in sum_dim / mean_dim

Completes the pairwise-summation arc started in commits 69fce3d
(sum/mean), cfde2de (norm), and 56a4a6f (var/std) by extending the
same precision-correctness fix to the dim-wise reductions.

`sum_dim_tensor_contiguous_{f64,f32}` previously ran a sequential
inner accumulator over a strided slice, with O(N · ε) error
accumulation. PyTorch's `torch.sum(dim=...)` uses pairwise
summation, so the prior FrankenTorch implementation diverged in
precision for any large `reduce_size`. `mean_dim_*` composes
`sum_dim_*` so it inherits the fix.

Two-tier implementation:

  * Fast path (inner_size == 1, the common shape: e.g.
    [B, D].sum(dim=-1)): the strided slice is pure contiguous, so
    pairwise-sum the slice directly with zero scratch allocation.
    This is the dominant case in transformer / attention reductions.

  * General strided path (inner_size > 1): gather each
    (outer, inner) slice into a reusable scratch buffer (one
    allocation per call, not per cell), then pairwise-sum. Same
    pattern as the var_dim_* fix from 56a4a6f.

Both paths inherit the O(log N · ε) bound from `pairwise_sum_*`.

Tests:
  * `sum_dim_tensor_contiguous_pairwise_fast_path_at_large_n` —
    shape [B=8, D=131072] reducing dim=1, validates per-row drift
    < 1e-9 on 8 contiguous 131k-element sums.
  * `sum_dim_tensor_contiguous_pairwise_general_strided_at_large_n`
    — shape [D=65536, B=2] reducing dim=0, validates the gather-
    then-pairwise path on 2 strided 65k-element sums.

Quality gates: 12-crate workspace test sweep — ft-kernel-cpu 309 +
ft-api 1091 + ft-conformance 101 + ft-autograd 314 + ft-nn 150 +
ft-optim 74 + ft-data 103 + ft-serialize 15 + ft-runtime 2 +
ft-core 70 + ft-dispatch 9 + ft-device 6 — all green; clippy + ubs
clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
